input.tenant_message: Ever since the previous tenant moved out I've been getting bitten by what I'm pretty sure are fleas — my ankles are covered in small itchy bites and I can see tiny jumping insects in the carpet when I walk through the living room. I don't even have a pet. I think the last tenant had cats and left an infestation behind. I've vacuumed multiple times but they're still here.

ai_output.recommended_action: Schedule a professional flea treatment promptly — flea eggs in carpet can remain dormant and hatch over weeks, meaning vacuuming alone is insufficient. A pest control technician should apply an insect growth regulator (IGR) combined with a residual adulticide treatment throughout the carpeted areas. Recommend tenant launder all soft furnishings on high heat and vacuum immediately before and after treatment.
